arguments.task: Continue the same browser session. Open and read only official Grain pages that directly support structured research fields. Specifically: 1) confirm pricing/access tiers on grain.com/pricing, including any API and MCP access language; 2) find the canonical official help/docs/developer page for Grain's API and read the authentication section; 3) verify whether the API is REST and whether both personal API key/token and OAuth are documented, or if only one is documented; 4) verify whether Grain offers an MCP server or only plan-level MCP access language. Return final extracted facts with exact page URLs you read. If official docs are missing, contradictory, JS-rendered, or 404, say so explicitly.

input.thought: Run a narrower continuation focused on canonical API/auth and pricing pages after the initial help-center dead end.

raw_result.description: AI meeting recorder and conversation intelligence platform for capturing, transcribing, summarizing, and sharing meeting insights.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official pricing evidence gathered shows plan-level references to 'Personal API', 'Workspace API', and 'Basic/Advanced MCP access', but no canonical first-party API auth page was successfully retrieved in this pass. Because the authentication method(s) are not directly documented in the evidence read, auth is marked as other pending human verification.
raw_result.gating_reason: Official pricing evidence indicates API access is tiered by paid plans: 'Personal API' on Starter, 'Workspace API' on Business, and higher support/access on Enterprise; MCP access is also plan-scoped on pricing.

api_surface.breadth: Official Grain pages successfully read in this pass support the existence of integrations, plan-tiered API access, and MCP access language, but no canonical API reference page was successfully retrieved to verify endpoint style or breadth.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official pricing evidence explicitly mentions 'Basic MCP access' and 'Advanced MCP access', indicating some MCP offering exists, but the exact implementation details or canonical MCP docs were not verified in this pass.

raw_result.blocker: Public pricing supports that API/MCP access exists, but lack of successfully retrieved canonical API/auth documentation in this pass prevents a clean ready_today determination.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: No public rate-limit documentation was verified from the official pages read in this pass.
raw_result.notable_signals: Pricing page explicitly surfaced tiered API packaging and MCP access; integrations and security pages indicate a mature product surface, but the attempted help/docs path returned a 404 during browsing.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Structured fields remain unsupported by directly read canonical API docs in this pass: auth_methods, api_surface.type, and buildability_verdict are uncertain because the browser evidence captured pricing and marketing/security pages but not a working first-party API/auth reference.
